skills.name: mckinsey-ppt-multidim-chart-expert
skills.description: Use when users need a chart strategy expert for PPT pages with complex trade-offs, such as multi-metric storytelling, combo charts, attribution waterfalls, growth-driver visuals, or uncertainty about chart selection for executive slides.

skills.name: mckinsey-core-insight
skills.description: Use when users need to extract a single sharp executive insight from complex data, define a conclusion headline, or convert analysis into board-level key messages for PPT pages.

skills.name: mckinsey-pyramid-principle
skills.description: Use when users need to structure a deck or argument with top-down logic, MECE decomposition, storyline hierarchy, or issue tree framing before writing slide content.

skills.name: mckinsey-slide-template
skills.description: Use when users need a standard McKinsey-style slide layout, page anatomy, information hierarchy, and title-body structure for business presentations.

skills.name: mckinsey-insight-extraction
skills.description: Use when users need to derive actionable insights from charts or datasets, identify key signals and anomalies, and translate observations into business implications.

skills.name: mckinsey-chart-classification
skills.description: Use when users need a taxonomy of chart families and want to classify business questions into comparison, composition, trend, distribution, relationship, or multi-dimensional chart categories.

skills.name: mckinsey-chart-type-guide
skills.description: Use when users ask which chart to use for a specific business question, decision context, or message type, especially for executive reporting and consulting-style storytelling.

skills.name: chartify-chart-generation
skills.description: Use when users need reliable code-based chart generation with Chartify for reusable visuals, automated exports, or implementation-ready chart scripts aligned with PPT storytelling needs.

skills.name: mckinsey-bar-chart
skills.description: Use when users need to design or optimize bar chart pages for ranked comparisons, gap analysis, or before-after performance views in consulting-style slides.

skills.name: mckinsey-combination-chart
skills.description: Use when users need combo chart design, including dual-axis, bar-line, or mixed-metric pages that show scale and growth or multiple linked signals together.

skills.name: mckinsey-donut-chart
skills.description: Use when users need donut or pie-style composition visuals to communicate share, mix, or structure in a concise executive format.

skills.name: mckinsey-grouped-chart
skills.description: Use when users need grouped bar comparison across segments, cohorts, channels, or periods with clear side-by-side contrast in one slide.

skills.name: mckinsey-multi-dimensional-charts
skills.description: Use when users need advanced multi-dimensional business visuals such as waterfalls, growth drivers, competitive switching, price-band analysis, or other complex executive analytics charts.

skills.name: mckinsey-stacked-chart
skills.description: Use when users need stacked chart design to show composition over time, contribution layers, and structural shifts while preserving total context.

skills.name: mckinsey-brand-health-charts
skills.description: Use when users build brand health or brand funnel presentations, including awareness-consideration-conversion, brand equity signals, and competitive brand positioning visuals.

skills.name: mckinsey-channel-strategy-charts
skills.description: Use when users analyze channel strategy, channel efficiency, mix optimization, acquisition economics, or online-offline performance for executive decisions.

skills.name: mckinsey-consumer-analysis-charts
skills.description: Use when users create consumer insights pages such as segmentation, retention, churn, cohort behavior, loyalty, or customer journey analysis for strategy decks.

skills.name: mckinsey-maternal-ecommerce-charts
skills.description: Use when users need maternal and cross-border e-commerce business charts, including category, pricing, channel, user, and operations analyses tailored to that domain.

skills.name: mckinsey-color-standards
skills.description: Use when users need McKinsey-style color system guidance, palette governance, and color usage rules to keep slides visually consistent and executive-ready.

skills.name: mckinsey-conclusion-first
skills.description: Use when users need conclusion-first writing for slide titles and narratives, turning evidence into direct recommendations and action-oriented executive statements.
